name: Must-Have Picadillo

ingredients.item: raw walnuts, soaked in water for 3 hours to overnight
diced mixed red and green bell pepper
Salt and freshly ground black pepper

method: Picadillo is a must-have ground beef dish for many Puerto Rican recipes, such as empanadas (see Pastelillos), and lasagna (see Vegan Puerto Rican Pastelón), or to eat on its own. This recipe is used many times throughout my cookbook, and it’s a staple you need in your home. This picadillo is made using walnuts and shiitake mushrooms, and the base is like a sofrito of bell pepper and onion, which is an ongoing theme in this book. This is the best plant-based version of picadillo. If you are allergic to nuts, use oyster mushrooms or your favorite vegan ground meat alternative instead.
In a food processor, combine the raw walnuts and shiitake mushrooms and pulse until the mixture turns into a crumble. Make sure there are no hard pieces.

In a pan over medium heat, heat your olive oil, add the bell peppers, onion and garlic, then sauté until fragrant. Add the walnut mixture, then add your tomato sauce, olives, adobo, Sazón, oregano, liquid smoke and salt and black pepper to taste. Mix thoroughly. Allow the mixture to cook, stirring, for about 7 minutes, until the “meat” is fully cooked and browned.
Once it is done, you can store it in the refrigerator for up to 7 days, or you can use it immediately to make your favorite dishes, I recommend making empanadas with the Pastelillos.
